params.text:
Progress Note - Respiratory Assessment Subjective: Patient reports occasional chest discomfort, currently stable. No acute shortness of breath. Objective: Vitals - BP 122/72, HR 72, SpO2 96% on room air. Labs - Creatinine 1.1, Troponin normal. Assessment:
1. Chest pain (R07.9) - Low acuity, cardiac workup negative (troponin normal). Evidence supports stable condition.
2. Hypertension (I10) - Well-controlled on current regimen (BP 122/72).
3. Long-term medication therapy (Z79.899) - Ongoing inhaler therapy needs optimization. Plan:
- SpO2 96% on room air indicates adequate oxygenation - no supplemental O2 required at this time.
- Inhaler regimen optimization pending - need to assess current inhaler technique and medication adherence.
- Follow-up coordination needed for outpatient respiratory evaluation.

params.message: Patient is stable with SpO2 96% on room air. Cardiac workup negative (troponin normal). Please coordinate outpatient follow-up for: 1) Pulmonary/Respiratory clinic for inhaler regimen optimization, 2) Primary care for hypertension monitoring (BP well-controlled at 122/72). Patient has active problems: I10 (HTN), Z79.899 (long-term med therapy), R07.9 (chest pain - resolved).

data.message: Patient is stable with SpO2 96% on room air. Cardiac workup negative (troponin normal). Please coordinate outpatient follow-up for: 1) Pulmonary/Respiratory clinic for inhaler regimen optimization, 2) Primary care for hypertension monitoring (BP well-controlled at 122/72). Patient has active problems: I10 (HTN), Z79.899 (long-term med therapy), R07.9 (chest pain - resolved).

params.plan_text: Discharge Plan: 1. Medications: Continue current antihypertensive regimen. Optimize inhaler therapy per respiratory therapy recommendations. 2. Oxygen: No supplemental oxygen required - SpO2 96% on room air documented. 3. Follow-up Appointments: - Pulmonary/Respiratory clinic within 2 weeks for inhaler regimen optimization - Primary care within 4 weeks for hypertension monitoring 4. Patient Education: Inhaler technique review with respiratory therapy prior to discharge. 5. Return Precautions: Seek immediate medical attention for worsening shortness of breath, chest pain, or SpO2 below 92%. Evidence Summary: Cardiac workup negative (troponin normal). BP well-controlled at 122/72. Chest pain (R07.9) resolved - low acuity presentation with negative cardiac markers.
